name: Generate HTTP requests from natural language

parameters.content:
Generate HTTP requests from natural language

Overview

This workflow converts natural language into executable HTTP requests using an AI Agent, an OpenAI Chat Model, and n8n's native HTTP Request node.

Instead of manually configuring HTTP methods, URLs, headers, query parameters, authentication, and JSON request bodies, simply describe the API request in plain English.

The AI automatically plans and executes the request.

Who is this for?

• Developers
• API testers
• Automation builders
• Backend engineers
• Anyone learning REST APIs

Features

✅ Supports GET, POST, PUT, PATCH and DELETE

✅ Generates request URLs

✅ Builds request headers

✅ Handles query parameters

✅ Creates JSON request bodies

✅ Supports dynamic authentication

✅ Executes requests using n8n's HTTP Request node

Requirements

• OpenAI Chat Model credentials

• API credentials for the target service (if required)

Setup

1. Configure your OpenAI Chat Model credentials.
2. Open the User Request node.
3. Describe your API request in natural language.
4. Execute the workflow.

Example requests

• Get current weather in Tokyo using OpenWeather API.

• Get the latest 5 commits from a GitHub repository.

• Search Wikipedia for Albert Einstein.

• Create a page in a Notion database.

• Send a message to a Slack channel.

options.systemMessage:
You never output JSON yourself. You always call the HTTP Request tool to perform the request.

For any request that describes an API call with enough information to build it (URL/service + method + necessary params), you MUST call the HTTP Request tool immediately. Do not ask clarifying questions unless the request is genuinely ambiguous about which API or endpoint to use.

Infer the HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) from the user's request.

Only include headers, query parameters, and body fields that can be determined from the user's request.

Never invent API keys, access tokens, bearer tokens, secrets, passwords, database IDs, account IDs, or other credentials, and never use placeholder values like YOUR_API_KEY.

If the request requires authentication and none was provided, ask the user for the missing credential instead of guessing or calling the tool with a fake value.

After the tool returns a result, summarize what happened in plain language for the user.

parameters.toolDescription: Execute any HTTP request to any REST API.
parameters.method: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('Method', 'HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, or DELETE', 'string') }}
parameters.url: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('URL', `The complete URL of the REST API endpoint.`, 'string') }}
parameters.jsonQuery: ={{ JSON.stringify($fromAI('Query', 'Return a JSON object. If there are no query parameters return {}. Never return null, empty string or text.', 'json')) }}
parameters.jsonHeaders: ={{ JSON.stringify($fromAI('Headers', 'Return a JSON object containing request headers. If no headers are needed return {}.', 'json')) }}
parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify($fromAI('Body', 'Return a JSON object. For GET requests return {}. Never return text or null.', 'json')) }}

assignments.name: user_request
assignments.value: Describe your API request here.  Example: Create a GitHub issue or Get weather for London or Create a Notion page  Credentials, IDs and request details can be included in this request when required.
